title: enchylaena lanata
intro: Enchylaena lanata, also known as the quandong or native peach, is a small tree or shrub native to the deserts of central Australia. The quandong is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its use in treating digestive issues, and is rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants. The health benefits of the quandong include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer properties. Enchylaena lanata is commonly prepared as a jam, syrup, or oil, and can also be used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
